sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content: The Certified Artificial Intelligence Associate (CAIA) is an intermediate-level certification that prepares professionals to design, implement, and manage AI-driven solutions in real-world settings. It blends theory with practical applications, emphasizing ethical AI, mentorship, and flexible learning modes (online or hybrid).

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 8–16 weeks (depending on pace and format)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Graduates of AI Foundation programs
  • Junior data scientists or ML engineers
  • Software developers transitioning into AI
  • Technical product managers
  • Professionals aiming to upskill in AI implementation

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Build and deploy real AI models
  • Master TensorFlow, PyTorch, Hugging Face
  • Work on end-to-end projects
  • Understand explainability and fairness
  • Prepare for AI/ML job roles

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Advance AI/ML skills
  • Build and tune real-world models
  • Deploy on cloud platforms (AWS/GCP/Azure)
  • Apply ethical standards in AI
  • Communicate AI's business value

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Advanced Machine Learning and Model Optimization
  • Hyperparameter tuning, ensemble techniques
  • Feature reduction (PCA, t-SNE), regularization
  • Hands-on: Predictive model optimization
  • Case Study: Credit Risk Scoring

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Project Portfolio: 2 major projects + 1 capstone
  • Quizzes & Labs: Per module
  • Final Exam: Online proctored (theory + practical)

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 3–4 months (intensive, full-time or part-time with labs and projects)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• AI developers and ML engineers
  • Backend/software engineers specializing in AI
  • Data scientists transitioning to engineering roles
  • Robotics, embedded, or CV engineers
  • Engineers seeking AI systems certification

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Master end-to-end AI pipeline engineering
  • Fluency with TensorFlow, PyTorch, Docker, Kubernetes
  • Real-world model deployment expertise
  • Design for vision, NLP, time series scalability
  • Prep for roles: AI Engineer, ML Engineer, Deep Learning Engineer

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Design, implement, optimize AI/ML models
  • Integrate AI into production software systems
  • Automate model training and CI/CD deployment
  • Follow software engineering best practices
  • Ensure model security, performance, observability

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: AI Software Engineering and Tools
  • Version control, unit testing, CI/CD with Docker
  • Hands-on: Build a clean, testable AI codebase
  • Case Study: CI/CD in Retail Analytics
Module 2: Deep Learning and Optimization
  • Custom models with PyTorch/TensorFlow
  • LSTMs, GRUs, Transformers, distributed training
  • Hands-on: Image Captioning Model
  • Case Study: Satellite Image Classification
Module 3: Computer Vision Engineering
  • YOLO, SSD, segmentation, model compression
  • Hands-on: Real-time object detection on Jetson Nano
  • Case Study: Vision AI for Quality Control
Module 4: Natural Language Engineering and LLMs
  • Custom embeddings, GPT/T5 fine-tuning
  • Deploying chatbots and Q&A APIs
  • Hands-on: Retrieval-augmented Q&A app
  • Case Study: NLP for Legal Documents
Module 5: MLOps, Deployment, and System Reliability
  • Airflow, MLflow, Kubeflow, Kubernetes orchestration
  • Monitoring, logging, model drift detection
  • Hands-on: Deploy scalable inference system
  • Case Study: Real-Time Fraud Detection in FinTech

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Project: Build and deploy production AI system
  • Technical Report: Architecture, CI/CD, performance
  • Practical Exam: Timed coding + debugging
  • GitHub Portfolio: Codebase, Dockerfiles, CI/CD logs

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content: The CAIP is a specialized certification for professionals seeking mastery in AI development, deployment, and leadership. It focuses on innovation, strategy, and ethical AI at scale, combining real-world projects with mentorship and collaboration.

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 3–4 months (part-time, instructor-led + project-based)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Experienced AI/ML practitioners and data scientists
  • AI team leads and technical architects
  • Innovation officers and AI consultants
  • Research engineers and PhD candidates
  • Tech professionals moving into strategic AI leadership

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Master cutting-edge AI models and tools
  • Strategic insight into AI adoption and scaling
  • Practical skills in AI product lifecycle and architecture
  • Build explainable, enterprise-grade AI systems
  • Lead AI initiatives aligned with ethics and regulation

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Design and train advanced AI architectures
  • Apply techniques in CV, NLP, and generative AI
  • Implement responsible, auditable AI systems
  • Deploy scalable AI with MLOps and cloud tools
  • Lead organizational AI strategy and innovation

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Architecting Advanced AI Systems
  • System architecture, API gateways, model pipelines
  • Hands-on: Architect an AI product
  • Case Study: AI Personalization in E-Commerce
Module 2: Generative AI and Multimodal Models
  • GANs, diffusion models, LLMs (GPT, LLaMA, Claude)
  • Multimodal models (CLIP, DALL·E, Gemini)
  • Prompt engineering, RAG, fine-tuning
  • Hands-on: Build a multimodal GenAI assistant
  • Case Study: AI in Creative Media
Module 3: Reinforcement Learning and Decision Intelligence
  • Q-learning, Policy Gradients, Deep RL with PyTorch
  • Simulation: OpenAI Gym, Unity ML-Agents
  • Hands-on: Train agent in simulated environment
  • Case Study: Smart Energy Management
Module 4: MLOps and Scalable Deployment
  • MLOps pipelines (Kubeflow, Airflow), Kubernetes
  • Cloud-native deployment (AWS, GCP, Azure)
  • Monitoring, drift detection, rollback strategies
  • Hands-on: Deploy CI/CD AI pipeline
  • Case Study: AI at Scale in Finance
Module 5: Responsible AI Leadership and Policy Integration
  • Fair, safe, inclusive AI design
  • Compliance: EU AI Act, AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001
  • AI for ESG & public good
  • Hands-on: Build a Responsible AI Framework
  • Case Study: AI Policy in Government

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Project: Team or individual AI solution
  • Documentation: Labs + architecture write-ups
  • Portfolio: Designs, deployments, codebase
  • Final Pitch: Live defense to expert panel

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content: The CAIC is an executive-level program designed for professionals leading AI transformation across sectors. It blends strategic consulting, risk management, policy, and governance to deliver real-world, ethical, and scalable AI solutions.

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 3–4 months (part-time, blended or online + project mentorship)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• AI/Technology consultants and advisors
  • Management consultants pivoting to AI
  • Digital transformation officers
  • Policy and regulatory specialists
  • Enterprise architects and program managers
  • NGO and development sector leaders

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Evaluate AI readiness and recommend action plans
  • Design implementation frameworks and AI roadmaps
  • Understand and apply global AI governance standards
  • Facilitate change and stakeholder alignment
  • Earn recognition as a strategic AI consultant

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Conduct AI maturity and readiness assessments
  • Develop actionable AI strategies and implementation plans
  • Integrate legal, social, and ethical frameworks
  • Manage AI transformation projects and engage diverse stakeholders
  • Deliver consulting engagements from diagnosis to impact

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: AI Readiness and Maturity Assessment
  • Frameworks: OECD, WEF, ITU, AI4D
  • Technical/data readiness, skills gap analysis
  • Hands-on: AI readiness audit
  • Case Study: National AI Readiness (Global South)
Module 2: AI Strategy Design and Implementation
  • Aligning AI with business goals and policies
  • Design thinking, vendor evaluation, ROI mapping
  • Hands-on: 3-Year AI Roadmap for SME
  • Case Study: Smart City AI Strategy
Module 3: Governance, Policy, and Regulation
  • Global policy (EU AI Act, AU Agenda, IP & data protection)
  • Ethics boards, risk assessment, compliance
  • Hands-on: AI Governance Toolkit
  • Case Study: Financial Services Regulation
Module 4: Leading AI Change and Stakeholder Engagement
  • Change management in AI adoption
  • Stakeholder consultation & awareness
  • Hands-on: AI Transformation Workshop Design
  • Case Study: Change Program in NGO
Module 5: AI for Development and Impact Consulting
  • AI for SDGs, inclusive AI, gender & indigenous data
  • Monitoring & evaluation in AI projects
  • Hands-on: AI for Good Program Design
  • Case Study: Agriculture AI for Smallholders

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Consulting Simulation: Live client engagement
  • Deliverables: Strategy doc, roadmap, governance plan
  • Oral Defense: Present solution to expert panel
  • Portfolio: Readiness audit, policy brief, client tools

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content: The CDSA is a practical, career-focused program that builds foundational skills in data science. It emphasizes statistics, data exploration, modeling, and communicating insights through hands-on experience with real datasets.

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 2–4 months (flexible pace, with guided labs and capstone project)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Beginners transitioning into data science
  • Junior analysts and developers
  • Business professionals expanding into analytics
  • Students and graduates from STEM/economics
  • Professionals reskilling from non-technical roles

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Master data analysis, visualization, and modeling in Python
  • Gain confidence working with real business datasets
  • Build a GitHub portfolio of projects
  • Prepare for interviews and advanced AI programs

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Apply statistics and data science concepts
  • Perform data cleaning, analysis, and visualization
  • Build basic predictive models with scikit-learn
  • Communicate findings with clarity and impact
  • Create structured, real-world projects

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Introduction to Data Science and Python
  • Roles, tools, and the data science workflow
  • Python basics, Jupyter, pandas, NumPy
  • Hands-on: Analyze sales data with pandas
  • Case Study: Retail Performance Dashboard
Module 2: Statistics and Data Exploration
  • Descriptive stats, probability, distributions
  • Hypothesis testing, handling outliers
  • Hands-on: Customer Behavior Analysis
  • Case Study: Heart Disease Risk Factors
Module 3: Data Visualization and Storytelling
  • Matplotlib, Seaborn, Plotly for charting
  • Interactive dashboards, visual storytelling
  • Hands-on: Build executive report in Jupyter
  • Case Study: COVID-19 Global Trends
Module 4: Introduction to Predictive Modeling
  • Regression, classification, model metrics
  • Train/test split, preprocessing, confusion matrix
  • Hands-on: Predict exam performance
  • Case Study: Loan Approval System
Module 5: Capstone Project and Career Prep
  • Complete end-to-end data science project
  • Present results and communicate insights
  • Build GitHub portfolio + interview coaching
  • Capstone Examples: Churn Prediction, Sales Forecasting

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Quizzes: Short tests after each module
  • Capstone: Real-world project with report
  • GitHub: Upload notebooks with documentation
  • Presentation: Demo or recorded walkthrough

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Data Science Professional (CDSP) is an advanced, project-driven program designed for experienced data professionals. It blends technical depth, strategic thinking, and domain-specific applications, preparing participants to lead impactful data initiatives in business, government, or consulting environments.

Learning Approach: Modular format with real-world projects, direct mentorship, and a capstone delivery model.

sections.heading: Certification Description
sections.content:
CDSP enables learners to design and implement scalable data science systems, covering everything from data ingestion to automated decision-making using tools such as Python, SQL, Spark, Airflow, MLflow, and more.

Duration: 3–4 months

Mode: Flexible pace, with guided labs and final capstone

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Data analysts and junior data scientists
  • BI engineers and analytics leaders
  • Data engineers transitioning into ML roles
  • Technical consultants in data innovation
  • Tech professionals aiming to lead DS teams

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Build optimized predictive and prescriptive models
  • Automate and orchestrate complex data workflows
  • Develop interactive dashboards and analytics apps
  • Gain domain-specific insights (health, finance, etc.)
  • Lead cross-functional data projects confidently

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Apply advanced statistical and ML techniques
  • Architect scalable, production-ready pipelines
  • Align data science work with organizational KPIs
  • Communicate insights effectively to stakeholders
  • Deploy and monitor models using MLOps practices

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Advanced Statistical Modeling & Machine Learning
  • Topics: Regression, Forecasting, Ensemble Methods
  • Tools: Python, scikit-learn
  • Exercise: Build a multi-model predictive system
  • Case Study: Customer Lifetime Value in Retail
Module 2: Data Engineering & Workflow Automation
  • Topics: SQL, Airflow pipelines, APIs, real-time data
  • Tools: SQL, Airflow, cloud storage
  • Exercise: Automate data-to-dashboard workflow
  • Case Study: Energy Consumption Forecasting Pipeline
Module 3: Data Visualization & Business Intelligence
  • Topics: Storytelling, dashboards, KPI tracking
  • Tools: Tableau, Power BI, Plotly Dash, Streamlit
  • Exercise: Executive Dashboard for Sales
  • Case Study: Government Budget Monitoring BI
Module 4: Applied ML & MLOps
  • Topics: Feature engineering, tuning, versioning, Docker
  • Tools: MLflow, DVC, scikit-learn, Docker
  • Exercise: Deploy and monitor an ML model
  • Case Study: Predictive Maintenance in Manufacturing
Module 5: Strategic Analytics & Domain Applications
  • Topics: A/B testing, risk modeling, personalization, causal inference
  • Domains: Finance, public health, development
  • Exercise: Solve a real domain-specific analytics challenge
  • Case Study: Public Health Targeted Interventions

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Project: End-to-end data solution
  • Technical Report: Model architecture, impact analysis
  • Codebase Submission: Clean and well-documented
  • Presentation: 5–10 min walkthrough or pitch
  • Peer Review: Evaluate a peer’s submission

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Data Science Consultant (CDSC) certification is a leadership-level program designed for professionals who advise, design, and manage data-driven transformation initiatives. This program emphasizes business impact, data strategy formulation, stakeholder engagement, and governance compliance, preparing participants to serve as data science consultants in private, public, or international development environments.

Participants engage in real-world simulations, policy integration exercises, and client-facing project design.

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 3–4 months (project-driven, part-time or modular format)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Senior data professionals transitioning into consulting roles
  • Management consultants specializing in analytics and AI
  • Policy advisors and innovation officers
  • Digital transformation leaders in NGOs, governments, and corporations
  • Analytics project managers and technical leads

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Learn to lead data maturity assessments and transformation planning
  • Deliver high-value, insights-driven consulting engagements
  • Navigate data governance, ethics, and compliance frameworks
  • Position yourself as a data strategy and analytics innovation expert
  • Build a strong consulting toolkit and case portfolio

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Design and lead organizational data science roadmaps
  • Evaluate data systems, teams, tools, and culture
  • Align analytics initiatives with business goals and sectoral priorities
  • Apply frameworks for responsible, equitable, and inclusive data use
  • Communicate technical value and ROI to executives and policymakers

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Data Maturity Models and Diagnostic Frameworks
  • Assessing data infrastructure, architecture, and analytics capabilities
  • People, process, and technology alignment in data strategy
  • Tools: DAMA-DMBOK, WEF Data Readiness, Data Maturity Models
  • Hands-on Exercise: Perform a full data maturity diagnostic
  • Case Study: Readiness Assessment for a Public Sector Agency
Module 2: Data Strategy and Analytics Transformation Planning
  • Structuring and scoping a consulting engagement
  • Formulating 1–3 year data science roadmaps
  • Building business cases and analytics investment portfolios
  • KPI frameworks and data ROI tracking
  • Hands-on Exercise: Draft a Data Strategy for an SME
  • Case Study: Analytics Roadmap for a Financial Cooperative
Module 3: Governance, Risk, and Ethics in Data Consulting
  • Data protection, algorithmic accountability, and risk frameworks
  • Responsible AI/Data principles (OECD, EU AI Act, African Union Policy)
  • Inclusion, fairness, and bias mitigation in data science projects
  • Organizational governance structures for data oversight
  • Hands-on Exercise: Draft a Data Governance Policy
  • Case Study: Data Ethics in Health Analytics for NGOs
Module 4: Leading Stakeholder Engagement and Data Culture
  • Stakeholder mapping and executive alignment
  • Cross-functional collaboration: IT, data, operations, legal
  • Designing data literacy and change management initiatives
  • Facilitating workshops and data strategy sessions
  • Hands-on Exercise: Design a Stakeholder Engagement Plan
  • Case Study: Data Culture Change in a Regional Utility Company
Module 5: Sectoral Analytics Consulting and Policy Integration
  • Data consulting in development, agriculture, health, education, and finance
  • Working with donors, multilateral institutions, and regulators
  • Data-for-development (D4D), MEL, and impact measurement
  • Integrating data science into national digital transformation agendas
  • Hands-on Exercise: Draft a Proposal for a Data for Good Program
  • Case Study: Data Science for SDG Monitoring in Local Government

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Consulting Simulation: Lead a mock engagement based on client brief
  • Deliverables: Diagnostic report, data roadmap, governance plan
  • Presentation: Pitch to a review panel (video/live)
  • Portfolio: Submit consulting templates, stakeholder maps, and summary decks
  • Peer Review: Evaluate and give feedback on another participant’s work

sections.heading: Add-On Options (Optional)
sections.content:
• Mentored Capstone: Work with a real organization (e.g., NGO, local startup)
  • Consulting Toolkit: Includes templates (e.g., proposals, maturity matrix, reporting)
  • CDSC+ Track: Additional training for public sector policy integration

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Data Analyst (CDA) certification is an applied, entry-to-mid-level program that prepares participants to collect, clean, analyze, visualize, and communicate data to inform decision-making. Whether you're starting your career in analytics or upskilling into a more data-driven role, this program offers the foundational tools and workflows required in today’s business environments.

This program blends real datasets, tools training, guided labs, and mini-projects.

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 2–3 months (flexible pace, project-based)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Beginners in data or business analytics
  • Fresh graduates and entry-level professionals
  • Administrators and officers seeking data skills
  • Finance, marketing, HR, and operations staff aiming to upskill
  • NGO and government staff handling reporting or M&E data

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Master core analytics tools used in the job market
  • Build dashboards and analytical reports
  • Clean, analyze, and interpret data for decision support
  • Create a portfolio of business-focused data projects
  • Prepare for data analyst roles across sectors

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Understand the role and workflow of a data analyst
  • Perform data cleaning, transformation, and exploration
  • Use SQL to query databases and summarize information
  • Build interactive dashboards using Power BI or Tableau
  • Generate insights and communicate them effectively

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Fundamentals of Data Analysis and Excel for Analytics
  • What is data analysis? Key roles and concepts
  • Excel functions for analysis: VLOOKUP, IF, INDEX/MATCH, pivot tables
  • Cleaning and formatting data in Excel
  • Creating reports and charts
  • Hands-on Exercise: Analyze a Sales Dataset in Excel
  • Case Study: Regional Sales Reporting for FMCG Company
Module 2: SQL for Data Analysts
  • Database concepts and structure
  • SQL queries: SELECT, WHERE, JOIN, GROUP BY, HAVING
  • Aggregation and filtering for business reporting
  • Creating views and basic stored procedures
  • Hands-on Exercise: Query an Orders and Customers Database
  • Case Study: Customer Retention Report for E-Commerce
Module 3: Python for Data Wrangling and Analysis
  • Introduction to Python for analytics (pandas, numpy)
  • Importing data (CSV, Excel, APIs)
  • Cleaning, transforming, and summarizing datasets
  • Basic visualizations with matplotlib and seaborn
  • Hands-on Exercise: Analyze HR Attrition Data with Pandas
  • Case Study: Monthly Employee Retention Analytics
Module 4: Data Visualization and Dashboards
  • Principles of effective visual communication
  • Power BI/Tableau basics: data import, calculated fields, filters
  • Interactive visuals, slicers, and storytelling dashboards
  • Sharing and publishing reports
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build a Sales Dashboard in Power BI
  • Case Study: Budget Monitoring Dashboard for NGO Program
Module 5: Business Insights and Communication
  • Framing business questions and KPIs
  • Insight generation and recommendation writing
  • Presenting findings to technical and non-technical audiences
  • Creating executive summaries and visual reports
  • Hands-on Exercise: Draft a Business Insights Report
  • Case Study: Campaign Performance Analysis for Marketing Team

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Module Quizzes: Tool and concept mastery checks
  • Mini-Projects: Applied tasks with real-world datasets
  • Capstone Project: Analyze, visualize, and report on a dataset of choice
  • Presentation: Dashboard walkthrough + insights briefing
  • Portfolio: Submission of Excel, SQL queries, Python notebook, and BI dashboard

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Machine Learning Associate (CMLA) is a hands-on, project-based certification designed to prepare participants for technical roles in machine learning and applied AI. It focuses on core ML concepts, model building, feature engineering, and performance evaluation using Python and leading ML libraries.

CMLA is ideal for learners who have a basic understanding of programming and data analysis and are ready to dive deeper into the algorithms and logic of machine learning.

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 2–4 months (flexible pace, project-based with optional mentorship)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Aspiring ML practitioners with Python and data skills
  • Data analysts ready to progress into ML modeling
  • Junior software engineers interested in AI
  • Graduates from data science or computer science fields
  • Professionals looking to build ML prototypes for their organization

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Gain confidence in using ML models to solve real-world problems
  • Master core algorithms for regression, classification, and clustering
  • Learn best practices for preprocessing, training, and tuning
  • Build a personal ML project portfolio for job interviews
  • Prepare for advanced roles or certifications (e.g., CAIE, CDSP)

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Understand machine learning concepts and taxonomy
  • Select, train, and evaluate supervised and unsupervised models
  • Apply feature engineering, regularization, and tuning techniques
  • Use Python libraries (e.g., scikit-learn, XGBoost) to build models
  • Communicate model insights and limitations

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Introduction to Machine Learning
  • What is machine learning? Overview and taxonomy
  • Supervised vs. unsupervised learning
  • ML workflow: data → features → model → metrics
  • Use cases: classification, regression, clustering
  • Hands-on Exercise: Train your first ML model with scikit-learn
  • Case Study: Predicting Housing Prices
Module 2: Data Preprocessing and Feature Engineering
  • Handling missing data, outliers, and imbalanced classes
  • Encoding techniques (one-hot, label encoding)
  • Scaling and normalization (MinMax, StandardScaler)
  • Feature selection and dimensionality reduction (PCA)
  • Hands-on Exercise: Clean and transform a messy dataset
  • Case Study: Preparing Data for a Credit Risk Model
Module 3: Core Algorithms and Evaluation
  • Regression: Linear, Ridge, Lasso
  • Classification: Logistic, k-NN, Decision Trees
  • Ensemble methods: Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM
  • Metrics: accuracy, precision, recall, ROC-AUC, RMSE
  • Hands-on Exercise: Compare multiple ML models
  • Case Study: Churn Prediction for Telecom Company
Module 4: Model Optimization and Tuning
  • Train/test/validation splitting
  • Cross-validation strategies
  • Hyperparameter tuning: GridSearchCV, RandomizedSearchCV
  • Avoiding overfitting: regularization and model complexity
  • Hands-on Exercise: Tune an XGBoost model
  • Case Study: Tuning a Model for E-Commerce Sales Forecasting
Module 5: Deployment Basics and ML Project Presentation
  • Model persistence (Pickle, Joblib)
  • Basic deployment with Streamlit or FastAPI
  • Communicating results: visualizations and reporting
  • Project packaging and GitHub portfolio tips
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build a mini ML app
  • Capstone Project: Predictive Model for Your Chosen Use Case (e.g., health, education, finance, agriculture)

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Module Quizzes: Theory and applied understanding
  • Mini Projects: Completed exercises and model notebooks
  • Capstone Project: Complete pipeline from data to deployment
  • Presentation: 5-minute recorded or live pitch of model and results
  • Portfolio Submission: GitHub or PDF with code, report, and visuals

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Machine Learning Scientist (CMLS) is an advanced certification for practitioners and researchers seeking deep expertise in the theoretical foundations, experimental design, and innovative development of machine learning systems. The program builds proficiency in ML theory, algorithmic design, probabilistic modeling, and scientific research methodologies, empowering professionals to push the boundaries of AI innovation.

Participants will engage in literature reviews, experimental replications, and novel model implementations.

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 3–4 months (research-intensive, with peer review and final thesis/project)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Experienced ML engineers or data scientists
  • PhD students or research interns in AI/ML
  • R&D professionals in tech companies or think tanks
  • University lecturers and academic researchers
  • Innovators building new algorithms or experimental AI models

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Develop expertise in designing and analyzing ML models from scratch
  • Gain hands-on experience in empirical ML research
  • Learn to write technical papers and contribute to open-source science
  • Master state-of-the-art methods (e.g., transformers, VAEs, RL, meta-learning)
  • Prepare for roles in ML research labs, academia, or AI innovation centers

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Analyze and improve ML algorithms through theoretical reasoning
  • Design and run experiments using reproducible science frameworks
  • Explore frontier ML topics like generative modeling, causal inference, and meta-learning
  • Publish results in technical format with reproducibility and code documentation
  • Lead advanced AI/ML R&D projects from conception to insight

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Theoretical Foundations of Machine Learning
  • Convexity, optimization, and gradient-based learning
  • Loss functions, regularization, and generalization theory
  • Bias-variance tradeoff, VC dimension, and PAC learning
  • Hands-on Exercise: Implement logistic regression and analyze convergence
  • Case Study: Comparing generalization across regularized models
Module 2: Probabilistic Models and Bayesian Learning
  • Probabilistic graphical models (PGMs)
  • Naive Bayes, Hidden Markov Models, Bayesian Networks
  • Bayesian inference: MAP, MCMC, Variational Inference
  • Hands-on Exercise: Implement a variational autoencoder (VAE)
  • Case Study: Probabilistic modeling for disease outbreak prediction
Module 3: Representation Learning and Deep Architectures
  • Autoencoders, embeddings, Siamese networks
  • Transformer architectures and attention mechanisms
  • Transfer learning and contrastive learning
  • Hands-on Exercise: Fine-tune a transformer on a domain-specific task
  • Case Study: Embedding-based similarity model for legal documents
Module 4: Reinforcement Learning and Decision Making
  • Markov Decision Processes (MDPs), policies, value functions
  • Q-learning, Policy Gradient methods, Actor-Critic models
  • Exploration-exploitation trade-offs
  • Hands-on Exercise: Train an RL agent in a custom OpenAI Gym environment
  • Case Study: RL for Energy Resource Optimization
Module 5: Experimental Design, Reproducibility, and Scientific Writing
  • Scientific method in ML research: hypothesis, experimentation, replication
  • Using MLflow, Weights & Biases, and Git for reproducible pipelines
  • Evaluation metrics for scientific rigor (e.g., ablation studies, error analysis)
  • Research writing and documentation best practices
  • Hands-on Exercise: Replicate a published ML paper
  • Capstone Thesis/Project: Propose, implement, and defend a novel ML approach

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Research Proposal: Formulate an original problem and proposed approach
  • Experimental Report: Design, run, and document reproducible experiments
  • Thesis Presentation: Oral defense or recorded video walkthrough
  • Peer Review: Critique and improve a fellow participant’s work
  • GitHub Portfolio: Documented notebooks, source code, and README

sections.heading: Optional Tracks/Specializations
sections.content:
• CMLS–Vision: Focus on ML in computer vision research
  • CMLS–NLP: Specialization in advanced NLP and LLM fine-tuning
  • CMLS–Responsible AI: Emphasis on fairness, explainability, and scientific audits
  • CMLS–Science4Dev: Applied ML research for development sectors (SDGs)

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Machine Learning Engineer (CMLE) certification is an advanced, industry-driven program designed to prepare learners for building, deploying, and maintaining scalable ML systems in production environments. It blends machine learning expertise with software engineering, DevOps, and cloud computing to prepare participants for real-world challenges in AI delivery.

This program bridges the gap between research and deployment, ensuring models work reliably at scale and deliver real-world impact.

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 3–4 months (modular, hands-on with project assessments)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Data scientists transitioning into engineering roles
  • Software engineers expanding into ML product development
  • Machine learning practitioners ready for deployment skills
  • AI developers building scalable models for production use
  • Professionals preparing for roles in MLOps or applied ML engineering

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Build and deploy ML models in real-world environments
  • Learn ML-specific DevOps workflows (CI/CD, monitoring, versioning)
  • Gain mastery in model containerization, serving, and scaling
  • Manage ML experiments, pipelines, and production systems
  • Prepare for roles like ML Engineer, AI Engineer, or MLOps Specialist

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Translate trained models into scalable, real-time services
  • Use cloud infrastructure and APIs to serve ML applications
  • Apply software engineering principles to ML system design
  • Monitor, update, and improve ML systems post-deployment
  • Build production-grade ML pipelines with reproducibility and security

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Machine Learning Engineering Foundations
  • ML vs. ML Engineering: Differences in focus and workflows
  • ML system design principles
  • Code modularization, logging, testing, and debugging in ML
  • Using Git and version control for ML projects
  • Hands-on Exercise: Structure a production-ready ML project
  • Case Study: ML Engineering for Credit Risk Classification
Module 2: Model Packaging and Serving
  • Model serialization: Pickle, Joblib, ONNX, TensorFlow SavedModel
  • Building REST APIs with FastAPI or Flask
  • Serving models with TensorFlow Serving and TorchServe
  • Handling batch vs. real-time inference
  • Hands-on Exercise: Deploy an ML model as a REST API
  • Case Study: Image Classification API for E-Commerce Platform
Module 3: Scalable Deployment with Docker and Kubernetes
  • Introduction to Docker: images, containers, Dockerfiles
  • Containerizing ML models
  • Basics of Kubernetes: pods, services, deployments
  • Auto-scaling and resource management for ML workloads
  • Hands-on Exercise: Deploy a containerized ML model on Kubernetes
  • Case Study: Scalable Inference for Video Surveillance Models
Module 4: MLOps and Workflow Automation
  • ML lifecycle management (data versioning, model tracking, reproducibility)
  • Tools: MLflow, DVC, Airflow, Prefect
  • Experiment tracking, model registry, and pipeline automation
  • CI/CD pipelines for ML (GitHub Actions, Jenkins)
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build an automated ML pipeline using MLflow
  • Case Study: MLOps for Predictive Maintenance in Manufacturing
Module 5: Monitoring, Testing, and Responsible Deployment
  • Performance monitoring (latency, throughput, accuracy drift)
  • A/B testing and model rollout strategies
  • Data drift and concept drift detection
  • Fairness, explainability (SHAP, LIME), and audit readiness
  • Hands-on Exercise: Set up model monitoring with Prometheus + Grafana
  • Case Study: Responsible ML Deployment in Healthcare System

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Project: Build, containerize, and deploy a full ML pipeline
  • GitHub Repository: Code, README, Dockerfiles, deployment guides
  • System Diagram: Architecture of your ML engineering pipeline
  • Live Demo or Recorded Walkthrough: Show working deployment
  • Peer Review: Evaluate another participant’s pipeline and suggest improvements

sections.heading: Optional Add-Ons
sections.content:
• CMLE–Cloud Track: Focused modules for AWS/GCP/Azure ML deployment
  • CMLE–Edge Track: Deploying models on embedded/IoT devices (e.g., Jetson Nano, Raspberry Pi)
  • CMLE–Security Module: ML system security, model hardening, and API protections

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Deep Learning Professional (CDLP) program is an advanced technical certification for individuals seeking to design, optimize, and deploy cutting-edge deep neural networks. This course immerses participants in modern deep learning theory and practice, empowering them to build scalable, high-performance models across domains like vision, language, time series, and generative AI.

Delivered through applied projects and guided labs, the program provides deep fluency in frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, Keras, and Hugging Face Transformers.

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 3–4 months (project-based, flexible learning schedule)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Data scientists and ML engineers advancing into deep learning
  • Researchers and AI developers building state-of-the-art systems
  • Computer vision and NLP engineers
  • Graduate students and PhDs in AI/ML fields
  • AI consultants delivering deep learning solutions to industry

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Master advanced neural network architectures
  • Apply deep learning in real-world projects across domains
  • Fine-tune LLMs and CNNs with modern best practices
  • Use cloud GPUs and efficient model deployment strategies
  • Build a portfolio of deep learning projects and APIs

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Design, train, and evaluate deep neural networks using PyTorch/TensorFlow
  • Apply CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, and GANs to real-world problems
  • Use transfer learning and optimization techniques
  • Build scalable, interpretable, and responsible DL models
  • Deploy models as APIs or web applications

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Deep Learning Foundations
  • Mathematical foundations of neural networks
  • Activation functions, optimizers, loss functions
  • Backpropagation and gradient descent
  • Model initialization, regularization (Dropout, L2), batch norm
  • Hands-on Exercise: Implement a deep feedforward network from scratch
  • Case Study: Predict Customer Churn Using Fully Connected Networks
Module 2: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
  • Convolutions, pooling, padding, and architecture design
  • Popular CNNs: LeNet, AlexNet, ResNet, EfficientNet
  • Image augmentation and regularization in vision tasks
  • Transfer learning with pretrained vision models
  • Hands-on Exercise: Fine-tune ResNet for medical image classification
  • Case Study: Real-Time Defect Detection in Manufacturing
Module 3: Sequence Models and Recurrent Neural Networks
  • RNNs, LSTMs, GRUs — theory and practice
  • Attention mechanisms and sequence-to-sequence models
  • Time series forecasting and NLP sequence modeling
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build an LSTM for stock prediction
  • Case Study: Text Generation with GRUs for Creative Writing
Module 4: Transformers, NLP, and Language Models
  • Transformer architecture: multi-head attention, encoder-decoder
  • Pretrained LLMs: BERT, GPT, T5, RoBERTa
  • Hugging Face Transformers, tokenization, and fine-tuning
  • Prompt engineering and RAG (retrieval-augmented generation)
  • Hands-on Exercise: Fine-tune BERT for sentiment analysis
  • Case Study: Custom LLM for Legal Document Classification
Module 5: Generative AI and Model Deployment
  • Variational Autoencoders (VAEs), GANs, and Diffusion Models
  • Model interpretability: SHAP, saliency maps, LIME
  • Model deployment with Streamlit, FastAPI, ONNX, TensorFlow Lite
  • Cloud-based GPU training and model monitoring
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build and deploy a GAN for image synthesis
  • Capstone Project: End-to-end deep learning system for chosen domain (e.g., agriculture, health, mobility, art, education)

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Project: Real-world application of deep learning, with a GitHub repo
  • Codebase Review: Architecture, optimization, documentation
  • Technical Report: Justify model design, evaluation, and deployment pipeline
  • Live Demo or Video Pitch: Present your deep learning solution
  • Peer Review: Evaluate a fellow participant’s model for clarity and reproducibility

sections.heading: Optional Tracks
sections.content:
• CDLP–Vision: Advanced deep learning in computer vision (YOLO, segmentation, etc.)
  • CDLP–NLP: Specialization in transformers, LLMs, and multi-language NLP
  • CDLP–Edge: Deep learning deployment on embedded and mobile devices
  • CDLP–Health: Deep learning for medical imaging, diagnostics, and bioinformatics

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Natural Language Processing Professional (CNLPP) certification is an advanced program for practitioners aiming to specialize in language technologies and AI-driven text and speech systems. This program provides deep expertise in building, fine-tuning, and deploying NLP models using state-of-the-art techniques and tools, including transformers, generative models, and multilingual AI.

The certification blends theory, real-world labs, and capstone projects to prepare participants for NLP roles across industries such as finance, health, education, customer service, legal tech, and more.

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 3–4 months (flexible, project-based with live labs or mentoring options)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Data scientists specializing in language problems
  • ML engineers implementing LLM-based products
  • AI researchers in linguistics and NLP
  • Software developers building chatbots or document AI systems
  • Public sector or NGO professionals exploring multilingual NLP

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Master NLP with both classical and transformer-based approaches
  • Fine-tune language models for industry-specific tasks
  • Work with African languages, multilingual text, or low-resource contexts
  • Build scalable NLP APIs and applications
  • Prepare for roles such as NLP Engineer, Language AI Specialist, LLM Developer

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Understand core NLP techniques and language model architecture
  • Preprocess, clean, and analyze text data
  • Fine-tune LLMs for text classification, summarization, and Q&A
  • Deploy language models with APIs and interactive apps
  • Address NLP fairness, bias, and multilingual performance issues

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Fundamentals of NLP and Classical Techniques
  • Text preprocessing: tokenization, stopwords, stemming, lemmatization
  • Vectorization methods: Bag of Words, TF-IDF, word embeddings
  • Named Entity Recognition (NER), POS tagging, text classification
  • Tools: NLTK, spaCy, scikit-learn
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build a text classifier with TF-IDF + Logistic Regression
  • Case Study: Email Spam Classification for Telecom Company
Module 2: Modern NLP with Deep Learning
  • Word2Vec, GloVe, FastText embeddings
  • RNNs, LSTMs, GRUs for sequence learning
  • Attention mechanisms and encoder-decoder models
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build an LSTM-based sentiment classifier
  • Case Study: Opinion Mining from Customer Reviews
Module 3: Transformers and Pretrained Language Models
  • Transformer architecture (BERT, GPT, RoBERTa, T5)
  • Fine-tuning vs. prompting vs. retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)
  • Hugging Face Transformers and Datasets
  • Domain adaptation for healthcare, legal, or education texts
  • Hands-on Exercise: Fine-tune BERT for multi-label classification
  • Case Study: Legal Document Classification for JusticeTech Platform
Module 4: NLP Applications and Deployment
  • Chatbot development using Rasa, OpenAI APIs, or LLMs
  • Text summarization, Q&A systems, translation, NER pipelines
  • Deployment with FastAPI, Streamlit, and RESTful APIs
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build a chatbot for a customer service workflow
  • Case Study: NLP-powered Knowledge Assistant for University Helpdesk
Module 5: Responsible NLP, Multilingual AI, and African Language Models
  • Bias, toxicity, fairness in NLP models
  • Working with Swahili, Yoruba, Zulu, Hausa, and other African languages
  • Transfer learning for low-resource NLP
  • Ethical considerations and explainability tools (LIME, SHAP)
  • Hands-on Exercise: Evaluate fairness in text classification model
  • Capstone Project: End-to-end NLP system (your choice: chatbot, LLM-based Q&A, document classifier, etc.)

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Project: Complete an NLP use case from data to deployment
  • GitHub Portfolio: Include source code, notebook, README, and API demo
  • Technical Report: Explain model selection, training, results, and risks
  • Presentation: 5–10 minute video or live demo
  • Peer Review: Evaluate another participant's NLP solution

sections.heading: Optional Tracks/Specializations
sections.content:
• CNLPP–LLMs: Focus on prompt engineering, LLM fine-tuning, OpenAI & Hugging Face APIs
  • CNLPP–Multilingual NLP: African/Asian/Arabic language NLP and low-resource AI
  • CNLPP–Speech + NLP: Text-to-speech, speech recognition, and voice interfaces
  • CNLPP–Legal/Finance/Health NLP: Domain-focused NLP pipelines

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Computer Vision Professional (CCVP) certification is an advanced, applied program for professionals building intelligent systems that can see, understand, and interpret visual data. This certification equips participants with the skills to design, train, and deploy computer vision models using deep learning frameworks, real-world datasets, and best practices in responsible and scalable AI deployment.

Through structured modules and projects, participants master modern computer vision techniques including object detection, image segmentation, tracking, and generative imaging.

sections.heading: Certificate Description
sections.content: Duration: 3–4 months (project-based, flexible or instructor-led delivery)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• AI developers and ML engineers specializing in visual data
  • Deep learning practitioners building computer vision products
  • Robotics, automotive, or drone engineers
  • Healthtech, AgriTech, or Smart City solution builders
  • Professionals in security, surveillance, and biometric systems

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Learn state-of-the-art techniques for image classification, detection, and segmentation
  • Build robust pipelines for real-time vision applications
  • Train and deploy models on cloud and edge devices
  • Understand bias, privacy, and governance concerns in visual AI
  • Build a portfolio with real-world computer vision projects

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Master end-to-end vision model development and deployment
  • Train and optimize deep CNNs and object detection models
  • Use transfer learning and open datasets for fast development
  • Integrate computer vision into production systems (e.g., APIs, mobile apps)
  • Apply responsible AI practices to sensitive visual data use

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Foundations of Computer Vision and CNNs
  • Basics of visual perception and image processing
  • OpenCV and Pillow for transformations, filters, and augmentation
  • Deep learning foundations for image understanding (CNNs, pooling, activations)
  • Hands-on Exercise: Image classification using custom CNN
  • Case Study: Plant Disease Detection from Leaf Images
Module 2: Object Detection and Localization
  • Single Shot Detectors (SSD), YOLOv5/v8, Faster R-CNN
  • Bounding box regression and anchor box concepts
  • Training and fine-tuning pretrained object detectors
  • Evaluation metrics: IoU, mAP, precision-recall
  • Hands-on Exercise: Train a YOLOv8 model on a custom dataset
  • Case Study: Helmet Detection for Workplace Safety
Module 3: Image Segmentation and Scene Understanding
  • Semantic vs. instance segmentation
  • U-Net, Mask R-CNN, DeepLabV3
  • Applications in medical imaging, satellite image analysis
  • Hands-on Exercise: Segment objects in medical scans using U-Net
  • Case Study: Land Cover Classification from Satellite Images
Module 4: Video Analysis, Tracking, and Real-Time Systems
  • Object tracking (SORT, Deep SORT), optical flow
  • Action recognition, pose estimation
  • Real-time streaming and inference using TensorRT or ONNX
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build a real-time vehicle tracking system
  • Case Study: Crowd Monitoring for Urban Planning
Module 5: Generative Vision Models, Deployment & Responsible AI
  • Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) for vision
  • Vision Transformers (ViT, DINO, SAM)
  • Explainability in vision models (Grad-CAM, saliency maps)
  • Deploying models via Flask, FastAPI, and Streamlit
  • Ethics and governance: facial recognition, privacy, and surveillance
  • Hands-on Exercise: Deploy a face mask detection model via API
  • Capstone Project: End-to-end vision system in health, agriculture, security, or retail

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Project: Complete computer vision pipeline with inference endpoint
  • Codebase Review: Includes training script, deployment code, and documentation
  • Technical Report: Detailing architecture, evaluation, and real-world implications
  • Presentation: Recorded or live demo of your application
  • Peer Review: Provide constructive feedback on a peer’s computer vision solution

sections.heading: Optional Tracks
sections.content:
• CCVP–Health: Medical image analysis and radiology AI
  • CCVP–AgriTech: Vision AI for crops, pests, and yield prediction
  • CCVP–Edge AI: Vision models for drones, mobile, and IoT devices
  • CCVP–Smart Cities: Traffic, mobility, security, and public infrastructure AI

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Cloud Computing Professional (CCCP) certification prepares participants to become cloud architects, engineers, or system administrators with the expertise to manage modern, scalable, and secure cloud infrastructure. Covering major platforms like AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud Platform (GCP), the program offers real-world cloud labs, architectural design exercises, and deployment simulations.

It aligns with global cloud career pathways while addressing local infrastructure realities, including hybrid and sovereign cloud adoption.

sections.heading: Certification Description
sections.content: Duration: 3–4 months (flexible learning with live sessions and labs)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• IT professionals transitioning to cloud roles
  • DevOps engineers and system administrators
  • Network engineers, software developers, and cybersecurity staff
  • Government and enterprise IT leaders planning cloud migration
  • Technologists in NGOs and startups deploying scalable platforms

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Learn to architect and deploy systems across AWS, Azure, and GCP
  • Gain hands-on experience with virtual machines, containers, APIs, and security tools
  • Prepare for vendor certifications (e.g., AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Azure Administrator)
  • Build skills in cloud-native development, automation, and orchestration
  • Address challenges in cloud cost control, governance, and compliance

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Understand the fundamentals of cloud computing, services, and delivery models
  • Architect secure, resilient, and scalable cloud solutions
  • Automate cloud infrastructure using Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC)
  • Implement cloud monitoring, backups, disaster recovery, and logging
  • Evaluate multi-cloud, hybrid cloud, and edge computing strategies

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Cloud Computing Foundations
  • History and evolution of cloud computing
  • IaaS, PaaS, SaaS: service models and use cases
  • Public, private, hybrid, and multi-cloud environments
  • Core components: compute, storage, networking, databases
  • Hands-on Exercise: Launch and configure a virtual server in AWS
  • Case Study: Migrating a local CRM system to the cloud
Module 2: Cloud Infrastructure and Virtualization
  • Virtual machines (VMs), containers (Docker), and Kubernetes
  • Elastic computing, auto-scaling, and load balancing
  • Virtual networks (VPC, subnets, firewalls, VPNs)
  • Storage options: block, object, and file storage
  • Hands-on Exercise: Configure a containerized application using Docker + GCP
  • Case Study: Scalable E-Commerce Platform on Azure
Module 3: Security, Identity, and Compliance in the Cloud
  • Identity and Access Management (IAM)
  • Encryption at rest/in transit, SSL/TLS, key management
  • Shared responsibility model and zero-trust architecture
  • Governance, compliance (GDPR, ISO 27001, NIST, local data regulations)
  • Hands-on Exercise: Implement IAM roles and policy controls in Azure
  • Case Study: Securing Financial Data for a Regional Fintech
Module 4: DevOps and Automation in the Cloud
  • Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, AWS CloudFormation)
  • CI/CD pipelines for cloud-native apps
  • Monitoring, logging, and alerting (CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, Stackdriver)
  • Serverless computing (AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Functions)
  • Hands-on Exercise: Deploy a CI/CD pipeline for containerized microservices
  • Case Study: Automating NGO Reporting System on AWS
Module 5: Cloud Architecture, Migration & Optimization
  • Designing for scalability, resilience, and high availability
  • Cost optimization strategies (reserved instances, rightsizing, autoscaling)
  • Cloud migration planning (6 Rs framework)
  • Disaster recovery, multi-region deployments, failover
  • Hands-on Exercise: Design a multi-tier cloud architecture using a case scenario
  • Capstone Project: Design and deploy a cloud solution for a real-world client (e.g., public agency, startup, or research institution)

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Project: Design, provision, and secure a cloud solution
  • Lab Evaluations: Completed labs with documentation (code, configurations, screenshots)
  • Cloud Diagram + Presentation: Submit and explain your architecture choices
  • Peer Review: Assess and give feedback on a peer’s cloud deployment design

sections.heading: Optional Specializations
sections.content:
• CCCP–DevOps: For cloud-native development, Kubernetes, and automation
  • CCCP–Security: For roles focused on cloud governance and cybersecurity
  • CCCP–Data & AI: Cloud data platforms (BigQuery, Redshift, Azure Synapse) and ML
  • CCCP–Hybrid/Edge: For organizations integrating on-prem and edge devices with cloud systems

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Internet of Things Professional (CIoTP) certification is a comprehensive program that prepares participants to work at the intersection of hardware, software, networking, and data in IoT environments. It covers the end-to-end development of IoT systems from sensors and microcontrollers to cloud integration, data visualization, and edge intelligence.

This certification emphasizes practical prototyping, security best practices, and real-world deployments using open-source platforms and cloud tools.

sections.heading: Certification Description
sections.content: Duration: 3–4 months (modular, lab-focused with capstone project)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Engineers and IT professionals transitioning into IoT
  • Embedded systems or electronics enthusiasts
  • Developers building smart, connected solutions
  • Smart agriculture, healthtech, or energy solution designers
  • Government or NGO staff working on digital infrastructure

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Learn to design and prototype IoT systems using sensors and microcontrollers
  • Build secure and scalable systems for monitoring and automation
  • Gain experience with real-time data streaming, storage, and visualization
  • Deploy IoT solutions with local (edge) and cloud-based architectures
  • Prepare for roles like IoT Developer, Embedded Engineer, Smart Systems Integrator

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Understand IoT architecture and its components
  • Interface sensors and actuators with microcontrollers
  • Connect devices using standard IoT communication protocols
  • Process, visualize, and act on real-time IoT data
  • Apply cybersecurity and edge computing in IoT deployments

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: IoT Fundamentals and System Architecture
  • What is IoT? Use cases and value chain
  • IoT architecture: device, gateway, cloud, analytics, interface
  • Sensors, actuators, microcontrollers (MCUs), and edge devices
  • Prototyping kits: Arduino, ESP32, Raspberry Pi
  • Hands-on Exercise: Connect a temperature sensor to ESP32
  • Case Study: Smart Greenhouse Monitoring System
Module 2: Device Programming and Hardware Integration
  • GPIO, analog/digital sensors, PWM, interrupts
  • Programming microcontrollers (Arduino IDE, MicroPython)
  • Serial communication and real-time data logging
  • Local displays and feedback interfaces (OLED, LCD, buzzers)
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build a motion-activated LED alert system
  • Case Study: Low-Cost Cold Chain Monitoring for Vaccines
Module 3: IoT Communication Protocols and Networking
  • WiFi, BLE, Zigbee, LoRaWAN, NB-IoT, GSM
  • Protocols: MQTT, HTTP/REST, CoAP
  • Publish/Subscribe systems and message brokers
  • IoT device addressing and security
  • Hands-on Exercise: Send sensor data to MQTT broker
  • Case Study: City-wide Air Quality Monitoring Network
Module 4: Cloud Platforms, Data Analytics, and Dashboards
  • Cloud IoT services: AWS IoT Core, Azure IoT Hub, Google Cloud IoT
  • IoT data storage and streaming (InfluxDB, Firebase, ThingSpeak)
  • Visualizing data using Grafana, Power BI, or Node-RED
  • Actuation logic and real-time alerts
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build a dashboard for real-time sensor data
  • Case Study: Smart Water Metering for Urban Utilities
Module 5: IoT Security, Edge AI, and Deployment
  • Common threats: eavesdropping, spoofing, firmware attacks
  • Security best practices: encryption, OTA updates, authentication
  • Edge AI: local ML on microcontrollers using TensorFlow Lite Micro
  • Deploying IoT at scale and managing devices remotely
  • Hands-on Exercise: Train and deploy a simple anomaly detection model on-device
  • Capstone Project: Design, prototype, and present an end-to-end IoT system (e.g., smart farm, home automation, asset tracker)

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Project: Real-world IoT solution with hardware + cloud components
  • Lab Submissions: Code, wiring diagrams, cloud dashboards, documentation
  • Architecture Diagram: Visual overview of your IoT system
  • Project Demo: Live or recorded video showing device data and system behavior
  • Peer Review: Evaluate a peer’s IoT system for functionality and design

sections.heading: Optional Tracks
sections.content:
• CIoTP–AgriTech: IoT for precision farming, soil and weather monitoring
  • CIoTP–Health: Wearables, patient monitoring, and health alerts
  • CIoTP–Smart Cities: Waste, traffic, and public lighting solutions
  • CIoTP–Industrial (IIoT): Manufacturing, energy, and predictive maintenance

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Blockchain Professional (CBP) program is an industry-aligned certification that provides participants with in-depth knowledge of blockchain technology, decentralized systems, smart contracts, and real-world blockchain applications. It empowers professionals to understand, build, and manage blockchain solutions for finance, supply chain, governance, identity, and more.

Participants gain exposure to platforms like Ethereum, Hyperledger, Polygon, Solana, and tools for smart contract development, DeFi, and tokenization.

sections.heading: Certification Description
sections.content:
Certificate Overview

CBP covers the end-to-end blockchain development lifecycle — from architecture and cryptography to consensus algorithms, smart contract scripting, and deployment. It balances technical skills with strategic insights, preparing learners for blockchain development, consulting, and innovation leadership roles.

Duration: 3–4 months (project-based, with hands-on labs and capstone)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Software developers and system architects
  • IT professionals exploring blockchain integration
  • Finance and supply chain professionals exploring tokenization and transparency
  • Government or NGO staff focused on digital identity, land, or registry systems
  • Entrepreneurs and consultants building blockchain-based startups or platforms

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Gain practical skills in blockchain development and deployment
  • Learn how smart contracts work and build decentralized apps (dApps)
  • Understand blockchain use in finance (DeFi), supply chains, and governance
  • Get hands-on with leading platforms like Ethereum and Hyperledger
  • Prepare for roles such as Blockchain Developer, Web3 Architect, or Tech Strategist

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Understand blockchain architecture, cryptography, and consensus mechanisms
  • Build, test, and deploy smart contracts on Ethereum
  • Design blockchain systems for real-world applications
  • Address blockchain risks, scalability, and security
  • Navigate token economics, NFTs, and governance models

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Blockchain Foundations and Architecture
  • History of blockchain and Web3 evolution
  • Distributed ledgers, nodes, peer-to-peer systems
  • Cryptography: hash functions, digital signatures, public/private keys
  • Consensus algorithms: PoW, PoS, PBFT, DPoS
  • Hands-on Exercise: Simulate a blockchain ledger using Python
  • Case Study: Blockchain for Land Registry Transparency
Module 2: Smart Contracts and Ethereum Development
  • Ethereum architecture, EVM, accounts, gas
  • Solidity basics: functions, data types, inheritance
  • Truffle, Hardhat, and Remix for smart contract development
  • Hands-on Exercise: Build a voting or escrow smart contract
  • Case Study: Crowdfunding dApp for Creative Entrepreneurs
Module 3: Blockchain Platforms and Enterprise Integration
  • Public vs. private vs. consortium blockchains
  • Hyperledger Fabric basics: channels, chaincode, identity
  • Sidechains, Layer 2 solutions (Polygon, Arbitrum)
  • APIs and blockchain integration with enterprise systems
  • Hands-on Exercise: Deploy a chaincode on Hyperledger Fabric
  • Case Study: Blockchain for Transparent Procurement
Module 4: Tokenization, NFTs, and Decentralized Finance (DeFi)
  • Tokens and token standards (ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-1155)
  • Stablecoins, staking, yield farming, DEXs
  • NFT creation and marketplaces (OpenSea, Rarible)
  • Hands-on Exercise: Mint and list an NFT
  • Case Study: DeFi Lending Platform for Smallholder Farmers
Module 5: Security, Regulation, and Governance
  • Smart contract vulnerabilities and audit tools
  • Blockchain security threats: 51% attacks, sybil attacks, reentrancy
  • Regulatory landscape (global + regional)
  • Governance models (DAOs, on-chain voting, treasury management)
  • Hands-on Exercise: Perform a security audit using MythX or Slither
  • Capstone Project: Design and deploy a blockchain solution for your chosen domain (finance, supply chain, health, government, art)

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Project: End-to-end blockchain application or smart contract
  • Code Submission: Clean, documented smart contract and frontend/backend integration
  • Architecture Diagram: Visual flow of your blockchain-based system
  • Technical Report: Detailing decisions, security considerations, and token mechanics
  • Live or Recorded Demo: Pitch and walk-through of your solution

sections.heading: Optional Tracks
sections.content:
• CBP–Finance & DeFi: For fintech professionals and decentralized finance builders
  • CBP–Supply Chain & Logistics: For transparency, tracking, and anti-counterfeit systems
  • CBP–Web3 & NFTs: For creators and developers building dApps and marketplaces
  • CBP–Public Sector & Identity: For governments, NGOs, and civic tech platforms

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Cyber Security Professional (CCSP) is a practical, multi-domain certification program that equips professionals with the technical skills and strategic awareness needed to identify, prevent, detect, and respond to cybersecurity threats and incidents. The program covers key domains including network security, cloud security, ethical hacking, digital forensics, and governance.

It is aligned with industry standards (e.g., NIST, ISO 27001, CIS, OWASP) and includes hands-on labs, simulated attacks, and threat response exercises.

sections.heading: Certification Description
sections.content:
CCSP is designed to help professionals secure information systems, digital infrastructure, and data in today’s dynamic threat landscape. The program blends theoretical knowledge with real-world scenarios using virtual labs, penetration testing tools, SIEM platforms, and case studies across sectors like finance, healthcare, government, and education.

Duration: 3–4 months (modular, with hands-on labs and scenario-based assessments)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• IT professionals and system/network administrators
  • Cybersecurity officers in government or corporate sectors
  • Penetration testers, ethical hackers, and incident responders
  • Cloud engineers and DevSecOps professionals
  • Graduates or career-changers entering the cybersecurity field

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Gain mastery over tools and techniques to secure IT systems
  • Learn practical offensive and defensive cyber strategies
  • Prepare for advanced certifications (CISSP, CEH, CompTIA Security+, etc.)
  • Build a cybersecurity portfolio including threat reports and configurations
  • Understand compliance and risk frameworks applicable to your region

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Understand cyber threats, vulnerabilities, and countermeasures
  • Secure networks, endpoints, cloud systems, and IoT devices
  • Apply risk management, governance, and compliance frameworks
  • Perform vulnerability assessments and incident response
  • Implement and audit organizational cybersecurity policies

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
Module 1: Cybersecurity Fundamentals and Threat Landscape

  • Types of cyber threats: malware, ransomware, phishing, APTs
  • Cyber kill chain and attack vectors
  • CIA Triad: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability
  • Cybercrime trends and threat actors (state-sponsored, hacktivists, insiders)
  • Hands-on Exercise: Simulate a phishing attack and analyze the payload
  • Case Study: Ransomware Attack on Municipal Infrastructure

Module 2: Network and Infrastructure Security

  • Network segmentation, firewalls, VPNs, and intrusion detection systems
  • Wireless and endpoint security
  • SIEM systems and traffic monitoring
  • Secure protocols (HTTPS, SSH, TLS) and port management
  • Hands-on Exercise: Configure a virtual firewall and detect malicious traffic
  • Case Study: Network Breach in a University Setting

Module 3: Ethical Hacking and Vulnerability Assessment

  • Penetration testing lifecycle (reconnaissance to exploitation)
  • Tools: Nmap, Metasploit, Burp Suite, Wireshark
  • OWASP Top 10 Web Vulnerabilities
  • Common exploits: SQL injection, XSS, privilege escalation
  • Hands-on Exercise: Perform a vulnerability scan using Nessus
  • Case Study: Web Application Audit for E-Commerce Company

Module 4: Cloud, Application, and Endpoint Security

  • Cloud risk models (shared responsibility, misconfiguration, data leakage)
  • Secure APIs, DevSecOps, containers, and Kubernetes security
  • Endpoint protection (EDR, antivirus, patching, asset control)
  • Multi-factor authentication (MFA), SSO, and IAM best practices
  • Hands-on Exercise: Secure a cloud storage bucket and audit IAM policies
  • Case Study: Data Breach from Misconfigured Cloud Bucket

Module 5: Governance, Compliance, and Incident Response

  • Cybersecurity frameworks: NIST, ISO 27001, GDPR, local ICT laws
  • Risk assessments and business continuity
  • Cybersecurity policies and awareness programs
  • Digital forensics, incident detection and response
  • Hands-on Exercise: Simulate and document an incident response plan
  • Capstone Project: Design and present a Cybersecurity Strategy for a Sector-Specific Organization (e.g., healthcare, finance, public agency)

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Project: Develop and defend a cybersecurity strategy with risk register and incident response components
  • Lab Evaluations: Secure configurations, scans, exploit reports, and firewall policies
  • Technical Report: Submit documentation on simulated threat detection and mitigation
  • Peer Review: Provide analysis on a peer's security architecture

sections.heading: Optional Specializations
sections.content:
• CCSP–Cloud Security: Advanced skills in securing AWS, Azure, GCP, SaaS environments
  • CCSP–Digital Forensics: Specialized training in investigation, data recovery, and evidence preservation
  • CCSP–Ethical Hacking: Full offensive security track with advanced tools and red teaming
  • CCSP–Policy & Governance: Designed for CISOs, managers, and compliance officers

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Business Intelligence Professional (CBIP) certification equips professionals with the competencies to analyze data, build dashboards, design reports, and support strategic decisions through data storytelling and visualization. It blends business acumen, analytics, and technical skills with tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Excel, SQL, and Google Looker Studio.

The program aligns with enterprise, NGO, and government data needs, making it ideal for those working in finance, logistics, marketing, development, or public policy.

sections.heading: Certificate Overview
sections.content:
CBIP is a project-based program that guides learners through the end-to-end BI lifecycle: data sourcing, modeling, visualization, interpretation, and communication. It emphasizes real-world applications of BI in dashboards, reports, executive summaries, and performance tracking, and integrates emerging trends like self-service BI, embedded analytics, and cloud-based BI tools.

Duration: 2–3 months (modular, part-time or full-time with a capstone project)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Data analysts, reporting officers, and business managers
  • IT professionals supporting reporting and dashboarding workflows
  • NGO, M&E, and government staff needing data transparency
  • Students or early-career professionals entering data & analytics
  • Finance, HR, logistics, and marketing professionals aiming to become data-driven

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Build interactive dashboards and automate reporting
  • Use SQL and BI tools to explore and visualize large datasets
  • Gain practical experience with cloud BI platforms
  • Turn raw data into insights that drive decisions
  • Prepare for roles such as BI Analyst, Reporting Specialist, or Data Product Owner

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Understand BI systems and organizational reporting needs
  • Perform data cleaning, transformation, and modeling
  • Design and build visual dashboards aligned with business KPIs
  • Generate reports with drill-down capabilities and real-time updates
  • Effectively communicate insights to stakeholders across levels

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
• Module 1: BI Foundations and Data Strategy
    • What is Business Intelligence? Components and lifecycle
    • Data to decision: types of BI (operational, tactical, strategic)
    • KPIs, metrics, OKRs, and data-driven culture
    • Self-service vs. centralized BI
    • Hands-on Exercise: Map a BI workflow for an organization
    • Case Study: Performance Reporting in a Development NGO
  • Module 2: Data Preparation and SQL for BI
    • Connecting to data sources (Excel, CSV, SQL, APIs)
    • Data profiling, cleansing, and transformation
    • SQL basics: SELECT, JOIN, GROUP BY, filtering, subqueries
    • Creating views and preparing datasets for analysis
    • Hands-on Exercise: Create a KPI dataset using SQL
    • Case Study: Sales and Inventory Insights for a Retailer
  • Module 3: Dashboards and Data Visualization
    • Visualization principles: clarity, context, storytelling
    • Tools: Power BI, Tableau, Looker Studio
    • Charts: bar, pie, line, combo, scatter, waterfall, maps
    • Filters, slicers, bookmarks, drill-through and interactive visuals
    • Hands-on Exercise: Build an interactive dashboard in Power BI
    • Case Study: Dashboard for Health Clinic Chain Performance
  • Module 4: BI Reporting, Automation, and Cloud Platforms
    • Scheduled reports, email alerts, and subscriptions
    • Cloud-based BI and embedded analytics (e.g., Google Looker, Power BI Service)
    • Integrating BI with Excel, SharePoint, web portals
    • Data refresh, scheduling, and data gateways
    • Hands-on Exercise: Set up auto-refresh and report sharing
    • Case Study: KPI Dashboard for Public Budget Monitoring
  • Module 5: Insight Communication, Governance & BI Strategy
    • Data storytelling and report presentation
    • Stakeholder analysis and tailoring visuals for different audiences
    • BI governance: access control, data stewardship, versioning
    • Building a BI roadmap and managing BI projects
    • Hands-on Exercise: Present a dashboard and insight brief to a non-technical stakeholder
    • Capstone Project: Design, build, and present a full BI solution for a sector of your choice (e.g., education, health, finance, agriculture, governance)

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone BI Solution: Full dataset → model → dashboard → story
  • Portfolio Submission: Report, dashboard link, and insight summary
  • Live/Recorded Presentation: Walkthrough of BI solution and decisions supported
  • Peer Review: Evaluate and provide feedback on another participant’s BI design and visual clarity

sections.heading: Optional Specializations / Micro-Credentials
sections.content:
• CBIP–Finance & Accounting
  • CBIP–Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E)
  • CBIP–Public Sector Transparency
  • CBIP–Marketing & Customer Analytics

sections.heading: Certification Overview
sections.content:
The Certified Digital Marketing Professional (CDMP) program prepares participants to plan, execute, analyze, and optimize digital marketing campaigns using a data-driven, omnichannel approach. It covers SEO, SEM, content marketing, social media, email, analytics, and digital strategy, empowering professionals to deliver impactful campaigns for brands, NGOs, startups, or public sector organizations.

Delivered through hands-on projects, platform walkthroughs, and case studies, CDMP aligns with global marketing standards and certifications (e.g., Google, Meta, HubSpot).

sections.heading: Certificate Overview
sections.content:
CDMP blends theory and applied practice, helping learners develop digital strategies and content plans, optimize online presence, and use automation and analytics tools to maximize ROI. It uses real-world case studies across sectors like e-commerce, education, tourism, fintech, agriculture, and public services.

Duration: 2–3 months (flexible, project-based with live or recorded tutorials)

sections.heading: Target Audience
sections.content:
• Marketing and communications professionals
  • Entrepreneurs and small business owners
  • NGO, government, and education outreach officers
  • Students and graduates entering the marketing field
  • Creatives, bloggers, or influencers seeking to monetize content

sections.heading: Benefits of Attending
sections.content:
• Build high-impact digital campaigns across search, social, and email
  • Develop a full digital marketing strategy from zero
  • Master in-demand tools: Google Ads, Meta Ads, Mailchimp, Canva, WordPress
  • Optimize content and ad spend using analytics and insights
  • Create a personal brand and professional portfolio for job or freelance work

sections.heading: Certification Objectives
sections.content:
• Understand key channels and tools in digital marketing
  • Develop integrated digital marketing strategies and plans
  • Build and manage SEO-optimized websites and ad campaigns
  • Track user behavior, measure performance, and generate insights
  • Apply ethical and inclusive digital engagement practices

sections.heading: Certificate Modules
sections.content:
• Module 1: Foundations of Digital Marketing & Strategy
    • Digital marketing landscape: trends, channels, KPIs
    • Understanding buyer personas and digital journeys
    • Omnichannel strategy and funnel-based planning
    • Digital branding and competitive research
    • Hands-on Exercise: Develop a digital strategy for a local business
    • Case Study: Multi-Channel Campaign for a Tourism SME
  • Module 2: Content Marketing & SEO
    • Content creation frameworks: storytelling, CTA, visuals
    • Blogging, video, podcasting, and short-form content
    • Search Engine Optimization: on-page, off-page, and technical SEO
    • Keyword research (Google Keyword Planner, Ubersuggest)
    • Hands-on Exercise: Write and optimize a blog for search
    • Case Study: SEO Strategy for Health Education Platform
  • Module 3: Social Media Marketing & Advertising
    • Organic vs. paid strategies (Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok)
    • Scheduling and automation tools (Buffer, Hootsuite)
    • Ad campaign planning (Facebook/Meta Ads Manager, TikTok Ads)
    • Audience targeting and retargeting techniques
    • Hands-on Exercise: Launch and analyze a Facebook/Instagram ad campaign
    • Case Study: Social Media Campaign for Voter Awareness
  • Module 4: Search Engine Marketing (SEM), Email & CRM
    • Google Ads: text, display, YouTube campaigns
    • Bidding, quality score, ad extensions
    • Email marketing tools: Mailchimp, Brevo, HubSpot
    • Customer journey mapping and lead nurturing
    • Hands-on Exercise: Create and schedule an email automation campaign
    • Case Study: Lead Generation for Fintech Startup
  • Module 5: Web Analytics, Conversion & Reporting
    • Google Analytics (GA4), Google Tag Manager basics
    • UTM parameters and campaign tracking
    • Conversion rate optimization (CRO) tactics
    • Building dashboards and client reports (Google Looker Studio)
    • Hands-on Exercise: Analyze website and campaign performance
    • Capstone Project: Plan, execute, and report on a full digital campaign for a cause, product, or organization of your choice

sections.heading: Certification Assessment
sections.content:
• Capstone Campaign: Real or simulated multi-channel campaign (strategy → execution → reporting)
  • Portfolio Submission: SEO blog, Google Ads campaign, social media strategy, email flows
  • Presentation: Slide deck or video summary of campaign goals, process, and outcomes
  • Peer Review: Review a fellow participant’s digital strategy and provide feedback

sections.heading: Optional Tracks / Micro-Credentials
sections.content:
• CDMP–Social Media Specialist
  • CDMP–SEO & Content Specialist
  • CDMP–Paid Media & SEM Expert
  • CDMP–Email & CRM Automation
